cases.transcript: What do you see going on in that picture? Oh, I see the sink is running over. I see the stool is tipping over. Little boy's trying to get cookies at. Little girl is reaching to get a cookie. Mother is drying dishes. Window's open. As I say, she's washing. Oh, she's drying dishes. Let's go look. And she's standing in the water. The cupboard door is open. There's a cookie jar. The lid is leaning against the cookie jar. Curtains on the window. I can see the grass and some shrubbery outside and the house next door. Okay. Kitchen cabinets. Is that it? I feel I see three people in there, a mother and a little boy and a girl. Okay.

cases.transcript: Tell me everything that you see happening in that picture. Everything that you see going on. Okay, start. Okay, a little boy is getting a cookie out of the cookie jar. He's standing on a stool that's tipped over, and he's handing his sister a. . . Another little girl a cookie, and the little girl is saying "Shh" because they don't want their mother to see. And the mother is wiping a dish, and she has the water running in her faucet, which is overflowing the sink and splashing on the floor. And she's stepping in a puddle, and she's wearing an apron. And outside is the yard with a path. And there are two cups on the table. And she's wearing an apron. And I guess that's it. She has short sleeves. There are curtains on the window. The cupboard door is open. There's only. . . There's a handle on one of. . . On three of the cupboard doors, but not on the rest. That's crazy. Okay.

cases.transcript: I was just wondering if you could describe everything that you see going on in that picture and just describe it out loud, and when you're done, just let me know that you're done. And the mother's sink, she's trying to do the dishes and the water's just rolling over and things are not too good at the moment. That's about it. Anything else? Well, there's water on the floor, I mean, you know. From the sink. Yeah. All right, perfect. Thank you so much.

The mother is drying the dishes, frowning but not turning off the faucet. Two cups on the counter, plate. Mother standing in the middle of the room. Curtains at the windows. Lid's fallen off the cookie jar. Cupboards half open. That's it, okay?

cases.transcript: Tell me everything that you see going on in this picture. Tell me everything that's happening there. Young boy is up at the cookie jar. He has a cookie jar open. He's reaching for one for himself. He's handing one down to his sister. The three-legged stool is coming down with him. They're nicely dressed kids, but the boy needs his socks pulled up. That's all I see there. Mother, I presume it's mother, is at the sink. The water is running over out of the sink. She's drying a dish. She's looking out the window. There are two cups and a dish. I presume that's a dish that are already dried, I guess, and sitting on the side of the sink. Nicely groomed. Nice curtains pulled back. It's apparently summer outside because the window's open and it looks like shrubs are growing. You can see a window in the other part of the house. I presume that's still their house. And there's a driveway bordered with grass. And there's a tree out there somewhere that you can see part of the trunk and part of the foliage. And I see cabinets around the kitchen, other cabinets. And that's about it. Okay.

cases.transcript: So in this picture, I was just wondering if you could describe everything that you see going on, and when you're done, just say that you're done. But yeah, just see if you could. Oh, okay, and then tell you what's going on. Yeah, tell me everything you see and tell me what, yeah, just everything you see in the picture. Okay. I'm gonna see if I put the light on if it makes it clearer. I don't know. Oh yeah, sure. The battery compartment fell out. Are you recording this? You should be. Yeah, yeah, yeah. Oh, good. Oh, the light does not. Oh yeah, the light's okay. The chair is falling. Boy, girl, mother is washing. I can't see too clearly. I know the mother is washing dishes, but I can't see too clearly what she's doing. Other than that, oh, she's standing in a puddle of water. And oh, the sink is overflowing. Okay. So now you want me to tell you what I saw? Yeah, yeah, that's perfect. Okay, it's a kitchen scene. We'll start with mother. Mother is standing in a puddle of water. She doesn't seem very organized, I have to say that. Washing dishes. And behind her back, the boy who seems to be the older of the two is standing on a chair which is falling, and he's reaching up to put things away, at which the younger, the daughter, is handy to him. With my limited vision, that was. That's perfect. Yeah, thank you so much. Is that it? Yeah, yeah, you're all set.

cases.transcript: It's a warm summer day. The kids were hungry and wanted a cookie. Mom was distracted because she had just received some bad news. The water is overflowing in the sink because her mind is focused elsewhere. Meanwhile, the kids decided to climb up to the cookie jar. The little boy went up first and handed down a cookie to his sister. Then he went up again to get a cookie for himself, and he's about to fall. Mom is not noticing because her mind is elsewhere. She will probably wind up taking one of them to the emergency room in a little while and make her day even worse than what she's thinking about right now.

cases.transcript: The little boy is reaching for a cookie and he's falling at the same time because the stool has tipped over. His little sister is reaching for a cookie and I think beginning to laugh because he's falling. The sink is running over and dripping water on the floor while the mother is trying to dry the dishes. The window is open and the curtains are blowing in the breeze coming in from outside. Water is running in the sink, that's why it's overflowing. I also believe that the mother is daydreaming, possibly worrying about something. That's it, that's all I see.

cases.transcript: Okay, picture. Mm-hmm. Um, boy standing on a stool getting a cookie out of the cookie jar. The stool is tipping over. His sister is reaching up asking him, she's asking for another cookie, and he's handing it down to her, and he has one in his left hand for her and one in his right hand for himself, except he's gonna fall down first. Um, the lid's off the cookie jar. The mother's washing, drying, she's washing dishes, she's drying a plate. Somehow she's gotten bemused and let the sink run over. The water's flowing into the sink. There are two cups and a plate on the counter. The window is open, and it must be summer or, you know, maybe autumn, because there are bushes and trees and things and they have leaves on. And it's in the kitchen. That's about, oh, the mother has on a dress that just about comes to her knee. She has an apron and a sleeveless dress. Little girls in a mini skirt and the boys in shorts. That's about it.

cases.transcript: Okay, the first word is pencil. Can you make a sentence for me out of pencil? The pencil was on the table. Okay, good. How about tree? The trees in autumn are beautiful. Okay, now I'm going to give you two words. Don't worry about the order that I give them to you. Just make a sentence using both words, okay? Child, hospital. Scampering. Scampering. Okay, let's try another one. Here's two more words. Cold, winter. Can you give me a sentence using cold and winter? Cold winter. Slush. Sleep. Okay. Now I'm going to give you three words. Again, I don't want you to worry about the order, but just give me a sentence using the three words. Here are the first three. Chair, doctor, sit. Okay? Okay, can you give me a sentence using them? Can you think of a sentence? Okay, how about these three words? Bureau, open, drawer. Can you give me a sentence with those three? Bureau, open, drawer. Right. And the, the other, chest. Okay, fine, thank you.

cases.transcript: Take a look at this picture and tell me everything that you see going on, everything that you see happening in this picture. Well, the little girl wants the cookie that the guy steal kid is stealing from the cookie jar with the lid off and he's tripping off the stool. The water's overflowing in the sink. The woman is drying dishes. There's a hedge outside and the letters are upside down. And there's a plate and a cup on the kitchen cabinet and a, well, that's all that. And the drapes and the sash window and the window on the adjacent part of the building. There's a path through the lawn. She's stepping, the woman's stepping in the water with slippers or sandals or something. That's about it.

cases.transcript: You see going on in the picture. Well, the little boy went up to get some cookies. The little girl's reaching up to give the jar to her brother. The mother is washing dishes, and the sink is full of water, and the mother is wiping the dishes. And let's see what else. And the boy is almost falling off this long thing here. What do you want to call this? A chair, a chair. And the water's falling over on the floor by the mother. And the little girl's handing her something to her brother. Cookies is probably cookies, or he's handing her cookies, whatever. Right? Okay, sure.

cases.transcript: Okay. Good. Pencil. I write with a pencil. Tree. The tree lost its leaves. Pardon me? The tree lost its leaves. Okay, now this time there's two words. I want you to put both of the words in one sentence. And you don't have to worry about what order you put them in. Okay? Child, hospital. The child is in the hospital. Cold, winter. The winter is cold. Okay, so this time three words. And you don't have to worry about what order they come in. Chair, doctor, sit. I'm in the doctor's office in the chair. Sit in the chair. Bureau, open, drawer. The bureau or drawer was open. Okay, good.

You only want action, you say? That's about it.

cases.transcript: And there's the picture. All the action. Okay. Well, there is a little boy who has climbed up on a stool to get cookies from the cookie jar, and his sister is asking for cookies too. His mother is washing the dishes, and the water is running over the sink onto the floor. Did I say that the stool was tilted? I don't think I have. Uh, cookie jar is open. The cupboard door is open. The little girl has two different shoes on. Can't tell if those are socks, is what they are. Uh, can't even make out what this is supposed to be. Little boy looks like he's wearing two different shoes, I think. I'm not sure what it is. Oh, and the stool is has like runs of legs. Is that all the action? I was just looking outside to see whether there's any difference. I assume that these are flowers. Oh, okay. Okay, good.

cases.transcript: 有有两个小美眉，她们在打羽毛球，打羽毛球以后很很高兴的打羽毛球。哎，那个突然靠右边这个美眉，那个有只小狗去也跟她玩，就咬了她的衣服。嗯。她自己就吓了一跳，那对对面美眉打球过来以后，她就也没有也没有接到。嗯，那相对的对面那个那个美眉看到小狗咬她，她也觉得很很好笑。哦，原来这只小狗是有一个小小男生牵着它的，嗯，一个牵绳，他不小心就跑到那个球场里面去去咬咬到那个小美眉。那里另外有个阿公阿嬷，他们在那个在那个应该是在在野外，哎，在那个在在在户外啦，一个凉亭，坐在那边喝茶，喝茶在下象棋。嗯。他们看到他们在打球的小狗咬她，她看到这一幕，他们也觉得很好笑啊。嗯。那另外还有两个小朋友，哎，我不敢走，可能是一个男生一个女生吧，在荡秋千，在一个树的旁边在荡秋千，还有一个溜滑梯，溜滑梯最后这这张哦，这张是一个一张椅一张长的靠背椅啦，木头的公园的椅子啦。嗯。这个图案里面有两棵树，还一点花，嗯，花草。

cases.transcript: 哦，这个这个爸爸在家带小孩，在烫衣服，这个小孩子就在玩插座，那个猫，那个猫就把那个桌上那一瓶水花瓶的水碰洒了，弄倒了，就是等于说狗嘛也在叫，爸爸很也在叫，狗也叫。爸爸一叫就把这狗跟猫都吓到了，所以说猫就不小心把这柜子弄倒了，这瓶水也倒了，倒下来了。这是爸爸在家，哦，还要讲日期嘛。礼拜天，爸爸在家带小孩，烫衣服，小孩没注意小孩去玩电插头，爸爸看到很吓一跳，然后大声一叫，就把这狗也吓到，猫也吓到了，猫不小心把那个柜子碰倒了，上面的那个花花瓶也洒了。

cases.transcript: 在夜市里面，妈妈带，妈妈，奶奶，妈妈带着小朋友去逛夜市。一个，有几个孩子在玩那个捞鱼的。哎呦，妈妈手上拿了一坨冰淇淋，还有几个小朋友在玩那个，在那个香肠摊上面玩那个西瓜拉。哦，还有一个，一个碰个小偷去偷人家东西。把人家背，后背包拉开去偷人家东西。奶奶带着，奶奶，妈妈。就玩那个捞鱼的游戏。好，这个是夜市。好。

cases.transcript: 在一个很很很繁华的夜市里面，有各种的小吃跟呃鱼玩乐。有阿爸带着小孩去去捕捉鱼，以后呃另外一个妈妈带带小女孩也去去做那个捉鱼的。呃去捞金鱼，然后妈妈一不小心她的下那个。她的帮小朋友买的小那个冰棒呃冰淇淋滴到了小女孩的头上。在另外一个她的姐姐很高兴的在旁边抓的金鱼很那个。在另外一边有一有有两个小朋友。呃妈妈带着小孩去买呃去玩那个掷骰子游戏。为得到的那个香肠的掷骰子游戏，可是他在呃他玩得很高兴的时候，他没有注意到他的背后有一位有一个小偷正在偷他的东西，所以他他的儿子看见了很生气的警告他妈妈说有小偷。这样的一个在夜市上的发生的事情，这是那个烤香肠，嗯，用掷骰子来得到香肠。嗯。

cases.transcript: 一一个妈妈，一个奶奶，他们带了带了孙子，还带了带带他们小孩子，妈妈带小孩子，是奶奶的孙子，一共三个人，一共加起来五个人。他们在夜市啊，应该在夜市啊，上面还有灯泡嘛，应该在夜市。他们在玩那个捞金鱼啦，捞金鱼好像就是要玩，玩得很高兴，哎。嗯，这个地方哦，他这，哎，不太对啊，应该是有有老老板在做这个生意啊。我看看，哦，冰淇淋，哎，奇怪，这不是妈妈吗？不是妈妈，妈妈在吃冰淇淋，怎么会不小心那个，她看那些金鱼啊，看看得太专心了，那个冰淇淋掉下来，掉到她她的小宝贝的头头头发上面，嗯。那么至于有有没有老板，我看不出来。还看到什么？没关系，哎，然后前面前面有椅子，还有这个捞金鱼的桶啊，或者是去那个那个勺子啊等等的。这一边就是在卖香肠了，对，卖香肠有三个小朋友，他们跟在这边跟老板在玩那个水水巴拉，是不是？哎，玩绳子了，绳子以后也就是大家来比输赢了，比输赢。哎，为什么绳子跑到上面去了？这个真的很奇怪了。嗯，那个那个下面烤香肠啊，正在烤，他这个碗啊，绳子啊，怎么会跑到上面？还有两只香肠，这个在不在那个理解啊？奇怪，哎，还有吗？还有吗？这个这个人那个，哦，对他做动作，这个这个是一个好像是小偷吧，嗯，他们玩得很高兴，他就把他的那个背包，背在背上的背包，那个东西把它，他大概偷拿吧，嗯，偷东西了，偷东西，为什么这个绳绳子在上面呢？很奇。

assessment.patient_only_transcript: 在一个夜市的夜晚，那非常热闹，有一群小孩子，有两个小孩子正在玩着骰子，玩骰子赌，哎，赌那个烤香肠的游戏。那结果其中有一个小孩子，他背包背在后面，就有一个扒手伸到他的背包里面要扒东西。另外一方面，一个妈妈拿着冰激凌要给小孩子吃，结果冰激凌都融化了。那这有三个小孩子在玩那个一个小水池，玩捞鱼的游戏。那一个妈妈在照顾小孩子，结果她的冰激凌都融化滴下来。那另外一个奶奶陪着她的孙子在捞鱼，那另外有一个自己在捞鱼。

cases.transcript: 这里面是在卖东西吗？哦，卖香肠。这是同一个东西啊。不是，呃，应该是啊。一个妈妈拿个冰激，在带着孩子们，还有一个婆婆带着孩子们到这个到这个钓鱼池来啊，捉鱼。就是在这块廊走廊正中间呢，就他们这个有一个摆的这个鱼，钓鱼可以。哦，钓鱼的池嘛。然后这个妈妈拿着冰激凌在吃冰激凌呢，掉了她那个孩子的手上。都在地上，冰冰都化掉了。对，就是说他们正在捞这个捞鱼啊。每天都在捞鱼吗？妈妈，那婆婆陪着她，她带着女儿，就在专心的在弄。就妈妈那个太阳，那个妈妈那个就一一直看他们，就是冰，这冰激凌就掉了，掉掉到掉到地上，冰激凌化掉。然后这边的人呢，他一个是在这个这个是他在烤这个香肠，烤香肠，然后一边跟那个旁边的孩子，两个孩子玩那个骰子，哦。那骰子怎么会到上面呢？然后趁着大家就在忙这边在抓鱼，这个地方在在摆弄玩骰骰骰子吧。然后这个小弟弟呢就偷他的东西，从那个手放进他包包拿东西。他一叫一叫，可能就把这个有人拿的东西，他就他就碗就掉这边来了。他本来这个碗里面有放香肠的。一紧张就是是不是丢就丢下去了，是不是这样子？也可以说这个这个卖的人一边在玩这个教人家玩这个骰子，一边在煎这个鱼，这个香肠嘛。因为他们都在玩的很开心的时候，他就是啊，就是。他可能有人，他就他后面他发现是不是发现他的，发现他就叫，然后他就这个这个把他吊上去。

cases.transcript: 就是有一个男人在家里烫衣，烫衣服用不到这个扇。嗯，他的小孩子很调皮呀，就要去把这个插头啊，那个手伸进去。这个狗吓一跳，就一直叫，这个猫也吓了一跳，结果就这个猫就弄翻了这个花瓶里面的水，这个水就是倒在这个毯子、浴巾上面。就是一个家里面的卧室，然后又挂有日历呀。小孩子大概一岁多，还吃奶嘴。

cases.transcript: 这是一个妈妈跟阿妈带了一个孙子啊，去街上玩。嗯，右边这个呢是在打那个洗巴啦，台湾叫洗巴啦，嗯嗯嗯嗯，在玩这个洗巴啦。左边这边的小孩子呢，这个老妈跟那个爸爸，嗯，就就在抓那个小鱼啊，嗯，用那个网子抓那个小鱼。结果呢，他妈妈的手上拿一个冰淇淋，不小心把它滴下来，滴在这个小孩的旁边。哎，那么这个呢，老老妈妈呢，她是跟她这个孙子在玩这个钓鱼，帮忙忙帮忙，这个小孩子拿那个那个小瓢啊，就在在在在挖，这个小孩子在挖。哎，结果呢，哇，洗巴啦这个东西啊，有三个小孩，一个小孩很不争很不争气，就看着他打洗巴的时候，中间这个小孩一个背包，他手上就进去把他偷东西，嗯，做那个做那个不正当的行为，嗯嗯嗯，这是，这最主要是这个题目就是说，这故事这个一个故事，这一个故事，这一个故事的时候，妈妈不小心把冰淇淋滴在那个小孩的头上，嗯，小孩子在在抓鱼的时候，结果呢，打那个洗巴的时候，三个小孩子一个不正当的小孩子伸手去偷人家的东西。哦，这，这是瓦烟枪的啦，嗯嗯，就是买买我们台湾那个那个那个街上那个买烟枪，烟枪烟枪打，嗯嗯嗯。

cases.transcript: The first word is pencil. You can write a note. Tree. The tree has leaves. They're about to fall down. Okay, now I'm going to give you two words and I want you to use both words together in one sentence. You don't need to worry about the order. First two words are child, hospital. The child was rushed to the hospital. Cold, winter. During a cold winter, you stay at home and don't go out unless you're needed. Now I'm going to give you three words and I want you to use all three words in one brief simple sentence. You don't need to worry about the order. Chair, chair, doctor, sit. We had to sit in the chair before the doctor came. Bureau, open, drawer. Somebody opened the bureau drawer.

cases.transcript: Right here's the picture. All of the action. And what am I supposed to do? Tell me all of the action you see going on in the picture. Oh, but I don't have to watch this. No, no. Can you tell me the action? What's going on in the picture? Gee, they're busy, aren't they? Yeah. Well, they're busy up there after the cookie jar. Okay. And they're reaching something up to it, I think they're. What else? What else do I go over here? Tell me all the action in the whole picture. Oh. Looks to me like she might be washing dishes here. Looks like she's drying a plate. And here it looks like she had a wreck here with a spill. Here's some dishes waiting to be washed. Here's a cupboard. Oh, over here too. Anything you see going on. Oh, did I mention the one set back for the cookie jar? Here to put some things up in the cookie jar. Oh, there's one, one after it, and then another one. That one looks like it might take a spill. Okay. Okay, good. Good. Really? Really? Here it looks like it's covered up with something in down here. Uh huh. Okay.

cases.transcript: 这个是在打羽毛球啊，两个打羽毛球啊。嗯嗯，啊这个是在干嘛？这个，这两个是在干嘛？在喝酒，是不是喝酒？喝酒啊，打羽毛球。嗯，啊这个怎么在狗在追他？那狗在追他，这个人牵个狗，狗啊在追他哈。嗯，啊他两个在打羽毛球，这个人牵个狗，牵个狗啊，狗在追他，啊两个在打羽毛球。在荡秋千，那个荡秋千，在打羽毛球，这个狗在追他，在荡秋千。

assessment.screening_summary: Patient speech shows signs of mild disorganization, repetition, and semantic vagueness during a picture-description task. While some scene elements are correctly named, the narrative lacks coherence, causal structure, and sustained informativeness. Examiner dependence and filler usage suggest possible word-finding or attentional difficulties. Not sufficient for dementia classification, but warrants further clinical review.
evidence_for_risk: Repetitive phrasing and lack of narrative progression.
Use of fillers and hesitations indicating possible retrieval difficulty.
Disorganized event sequencing without clear causal or spatial links.
Examiner-dependent responses rather than self-initiated coherent description.
evidence_against_risk: Patient correctly identifies multiple visible elements (badminton, dog, swing, drinking).
Speech is not entirely unintelligible
basic scene elements are recognizable.
No explicit memory complaint or caregiver concern reported in audio.
alternative_explanations: Possible fatigue, distraction, or mild anxiety affecting task performance.
Language processing or attentional load may be higher in multi-scene description task.
May be a non-clinical response to a complex visual prompt without cognitive impairment.
risk_control_suggestions: Administer formal cognitive screening battery (e.g., MoCA, MMSE) in clinic setting.
Observe patient during structured tasks with increasing complexity.
Consider caregiver report or collateral history to contextualize speech patterns.
assessment.visit_recommendation: Prompt clinical evaluation with formal cognitive screening and functional assessment.
